'''Teh'Jar II''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that occupied the second [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="HR" /> in the [[Teh'Jar system/Legends|Teh'Jar system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']]<ref name="EAOC" /> [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] began to use [[Weapon/Legends|armed]] patrols to enforce a high technology ban there.<ref name="HR" />

Around the same [[Time/Legends|time]], the rivals [[Xenoarchaeology/Legends|xenoarchaeologist]] [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Angrail Ryhl]] and the [[Antiques dealer/Legends|antiquities dealer]] [[Nardo Sau]] were both searching for the [[Um'Tal]], or "Sky Stone," an ancient artifact that originated with a lost civilization and was believed to possess mystical powers. Their search led both [[Gender/Legends|men]] to Teh'Jar II, with Ryhl hiring the best fixer in the Minos Cluster to help him deal with the Imperial technology ban.<ref name="HR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Teh'Jar II was mentioned in the [[1995]] sourcebook ''[[Heroes & Rogues]]'', authored by [[Rick D. Stuart|Rick Stuart]] and [[Paul Sudlow]] and published for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="HR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Teh'Jar [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Teh'Jar II, in grid square M-20.<ref name="TEA" />
